question_text: The Earth orbits the Sun once every 365 days. Mercury orbits the Sun once every 88 days. Which statement best explains why Mercury takes less time to orbit the Sun?

options.label: A
options.text: Mercury is much closer to the Sun, so it has a shorter orbital path and moves faster

options.label: B
options.text: Mercury is smaller than Earth, so it doesn't need as much time to orbit

options.label: C
options.text: Mercury orbits faster because it is hotter than Earth

options.label: D
options.text: Mercury has no moons, which makes it orbit the Sun more quickly

explanation: Mercury orbits much closer to the Sun than Earth, giving it a much shorter orbital path. Even though Mercury also moves faster due to the Sun's stronger gravity at that distance, the combination of a shorter path and faster speed means Mercury completes its orbit in just 88 days.
